title: draba micheorum
intro: Draba micheorum, also known as Micheor's whitlow grass, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds that may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Draba micheorum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against infection. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried and made into a tea or infused in oil to be used topically for its medicinal properties.
